steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The blank describes the role birds' respiratory systems play for paleontologists studying extinct theropods and sauropods. The decisive clue is that modern birds have these same air-sac structures, so paleontologists use birds as stand-ins to understand the extinct animals' anatomy.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "harbingers of": A harbinger signals something coming in the future. Birds' respiratory systems don't predict or foreshadow those of extinct animals—the extinct animals came first. ✗
  • "emissaries for": An emissary is a diplomatic representative sent between parties. Birds are not acting as messengers on behalf of extinct animals; this word misapplies a political/diplomatic concept to a scientific context. ✗
  • "subordinates of": Subordinate implies a lower-ranking member of a hierarchy. Nothing in the passage suggests birds are inferior to or ranked below the extinct animals; the relationship is one of similarity used for inference. ✗
  • "proxies for": A proxy is a stand-in or substitute. Paleontologists treat modern birds' respiratory systems as stand-ins for those of the extinct animals, using them to infer what the extinct anatomy looked like. This fits precisely. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
